g.title: /bip.ms — Cold-start dashboard for a manuscript session. Scans tracked EPICs across code repos for new results, figures, and findings.
/bip.ms.poll — Mid-session update: pulls remote repos, runs make targets, checks for new EPIC results. Notifies via ntfy when findings arrive.
High-level discussion with Claude about new findings from EPIC workers: meaning, implications, and what to do next. Outcomes are filed as new GitHub issues via /bip.issue.file.
/bip.lit — Unified skill for literature search: ASTA for external Semantic Scholar corpus, local search for the ~6k paper JSONL library.
/bip.issue.file — Create or update a GitHub issue from a local markdown file. Discussion and literature search both feed the issue draft.
/bip.issue.check — Validate issue drafts against CONSTITUTION.md for completeness, scope discipline, and correctness brainstorming before submission.
CONSTITUTION.md — Development principles that bip.issue.check validates against: scope discipline, file-based state, skill-first design.

svg.text: Remote Conductor · code repos on compute server

g.title: /bip.epic — Cold-start dashboard for EPIC orchestration. Scans clones, GitHub issues, and EPIC status to establish full context.
/bip.epic.poll — Mid-session scan of GitHub activity and clone status. Performs housekeeping (prune merged branches, update status).
/bip.epic.spawn — Spawn a Claude Code worker in a dedicated tmux window for a GitHub issue. Each worker gets its own clone or worktree.
/bip.epic.handoff — Worker self-spawns a follow-up issue and hands off to a new slot when it finishes or discovers adjacent work.
Clone α — Autonomous Claude Code worker running /bip.issue.work. Implements, tests, and creates a PR for one GitHub issue.
Clone β — Autonomous Claude Code worker running /bip.issue.work in a separate tmux window and git clone.
Clone γ — Autonomous Claude Code worker running /bip.issue.work in a separate tmux window and git clone.
@issue-lead — Fresh-eyes subagent that evaluates worker progress from file-based state. Nudges workers forward or escalates to the human.
@surprising-conclusion-skeptic — Checks for bugs, unfair comparisons, and confounded measurements before accepting surprising or strong results.
/bip.pr.review — Comprehensive pre-merge quality checklist: spec compliance, tests, static analysis, doc sync.
/bip.land — Land a PR branch: squash merge, clean up local and remote branches, return to main.
/bip.issue.next — Draft a follow-up GitHub issue from a PR decision or identified gap. Many landed PRs surface adjacent work that needs filing.
/bip.kaizen — After any friction, reflect and propose concrete improvements to skills, CLAUDE.md, docs, or workflow.
g.text: /bip.kaizen · reflect on friction → improve skills, docs, workflow
